task:
Ticket 0064 — Atom 1 of a multi-atom fix: guarantee the Oz daemon ACTUALLY EXITS on shutdown/reload even when a dashboard SSE/keep-alive stream is still open. This is the root of the zombie bug.

Problem (verified by Oscar): `packages/daemon/bin/oz.mjs` `shutdown()` calls `oz.close()`, and `OzServer.close()` in `packages/daemon/src/server.ts` (line ~238) does `server.close(() => { ctx.store.close(); resolve() })`. Node's `http.Server.close(cb)` only invokes its callback once ALL open connections have ended. The Electron dashboard holds an open SSE/event stream, so the callback never fires, `process.exit(0)` never runs, and the old process lingers forever as a zombie still bound to the dashboard. SIGTERM hits the very same wedged path, so it cannot kill it either (only SIGKILL did, in the incident).

Make these changes:

1. `packages/daemon/src/server.ts` — in the returned `close()`: after calling `server.close(resolveCallback)`, IMMEDIATELY force-terminate lingering sockets so the callback can fire. Node 18.2+/25 exposes `server.closeAllConnections()` — call it right after `server.close(...)` (guard with a `typeof` check for safety, but it exists on Node 25 which this runs on). This drains HTTP keep-alive AND the SSE/event streams. Keep the existing `ctx.store.close()` in the callback with its try/catch. The promise must now resolve promptly even with an open client connection.

2. `packages/daemon/bin/oz.mjs` — harden `shutdown()`:
   - Make it idempotent: a second SIGINT/SIGTERM while shutdown is already in flight must NOT start a second close (guard with a module-level `let shuttingDown = false`).
   - Add a HARD force-exit watchdog: when shutdown begins, start a timer (e.g. 4000ms) that calls `process.exit(1)` unconditionally — the SIGKILL-self fallback the ticket requires, so the process can NEVER linger even if `oz.close()` hangs for any reason. Call `.unref()` on the timer so it does not by itself keep the event loop alive. Keep the existing `oz.close().finally(() => process.exit(0))` for the clean path; the watchdog only fires if the clean path didn't.

Acceptance criteria:
- A new/extended test in `packages/daemon/tests/` proves `createOzServer(...).close()` RESOLVES even when a client connection is still open against the server (open a real socket/HTTP request to the bound port that stays open, then assert `close()` resolves without hanging). Use an ephemeral port (`port: 0`) and the existing test injection options (inject `store`, `restartDaemon`, etc. as the other daemon tests do — look at `packages/daemon/tests/daemon-auto-reload.test.ts` and `read-surfaces.test.ts` for the established setup pattern, including injected `restartDaemon`/`buildDaemonForReload` no-ops so nothing shells out).
- Do NOT change reload scheduling, the stale-detection logic, or `oz.sh` in this atom — those are later atoms. Touch only the close/exit path and its test.

Write-scope (do not touch anything else): `packages/daemon/src/server.ts`, `packages/daemon/bin/oz.mjs`, `packages/daemon/tests/**`.

Must not break: existing daemon tests must stay green; the normal clean shutdown (no open streams) must still `process.exit(0)`.

Exit criterion (run these and paste output): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` is green (including the new close-with-open-connection test) and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck` passes.

Ticket 0064 — Atom 2 (final required item): add the regression repro the ticket mandates — a PROCESS-LEVEL test proving the real daemon actually EXITS on shutdown even with a dashboard stream open, so it can never linger as the zombie from the 2026-06-25 incident.

Why this is needed even though component tests exist: atom 0 already added `tests/server-close.test.ts` (close() resolves with an open SSE client), `tests/events.test.ts` (shutdown closes streams), and `tests/oz-script.test.ts` (SOURCE assertions that bin/oz.mjs has the SIGKILL watchdog + idempotent guard, and oz.sh reaps by pattern). What NONE of them do is spawn the REAL `packages/daemon/bin/oz.mjs` process and prove the actual SIGTERM→exit path works end-to-end. The incident was a real process that would not die; only a real-process test proves the fix.

Add a new test file `packages/daemon/tests/daemon-exit-repro.test.ts` that:
1. Spawns the real daemon binary: `node packages/daemon/bin/oz.mjs --port <freePort>` with `cwd` set to a fresh temp install fixture. Reuse `writeInstallFixture(home, ['cocoder'])` from `tests/helpers/daemon-reload-fixture.ts` to lay down a valid cocoderHome (it creates the `local/` layout the daemon boots against). Pick a concrete free port (e.g. bind a throwaway `net` server to :0, read the assigned port, close it, reuse that number) — do not use 0 for the spawned bin since the test must reach it by URL.
2. Waits for the daemon to be up by polling `GET http://127.0.0.1:<port>/health` with a BOUNDED deadline (e.g. up to 10s, then fail with a clear message — never an unbounded/infinite poll). If it cannot come up in the sandbox, fail loudly rather than hang.
3. Opens a real SSE stream to `/oz/events` (Bearer from `GET /auth/session`, mirroring the `openSse` helper pattern in `tests/server-close.test.ts`/`tests/events.test.ts`) and confirms it received `: connected`/a frame — i.e. a live dashboard-style stream is genuinely held open.
4. Sends `SIGTERM` to the child and asserts the child process EMITS its `exit` event within a bounded window (e.g. <= 5000ms — the bin's force-exit watchdog is 2500ms). This is the core repro: an open stream must NOT keep the old process alive. Assert it exited (exit/close event fired); the exit code may be 0 (clean) or the watchdog code — accept either, the point is that it died.
5. After exit, prove no zombie holds the port: assert that a fresh `net` server can immediately bind the same port (i.e. the port is free / nothing survives on it). This is the test-faithful stand-in for the ticket's 'no surviving oz.mjs besides the new listener'.
6. TEARDOWN MUST BE BULLETPROOF: in `afterEach`/`finally`, if the child is still alive, `child.kill('SIGKILL')` it (and best-effort reap any stray), and `rm` the temp home. The test must never leak a real running daemon on the dev machine. Give the test a generous per-test timeout (e.g. 20000ms) so a slow boot doesn't flake as a false failure.

Notes / constraints:
- The bin boots with `warmCliCacheOnBoot:true`, which fires background CLI probes; that is fine — tolerate it, do NOT edit the bin to disable it (out of scope). Just ensure teardown kills the child so nothing lingers.
- Do NOT modify `bin/oz.mjs`, `server.ts`, `oz.sh`, or any non-test source — this atom is test-only.
- Match the existing test style (vitest, the fixture helpers, the `openSse`/`waitFor`/`withTimeout` helper shapes already in `tests/server-close.test.ts`).

Write-scope (test-only): `packages/daemon/tests/**`.

Must not break: the full daemon suite stays green; the new test is deterministic within its bounded deadlines (no infinite polls, guaranteed child teardown).

Exit criterion (run and paste output): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` is green INCLUDING the new `daemon-exit-repro` test, and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck` passes. In your summary, paste the spawn→SIGTERM→exit timing the test observed (proving the real process died within the watchdog window).
